items.title: Alphabet's Gemini delay, spending worries loom over earnings
items.summary: Alphabet faces heightened scrutiny from investors as a delay in the launch of a model key to its AI ‌ambitions adds to worries over the payoff from massive data-center spending, months after raising ‌expectations with a blockbuster quarter for cloud sales.  The Google parent — set to report second-quarter results on Wednesday — has delayed from ​June the launch of its next flagship model, Gemini 3.5 Pro, built especially to catch up with rivals in the lucrative market for AI coding tools and agentic AI tasks.  "While Google is missing the boat on AI coding and that's a very real growing concern ... Google's strategy is ⁠all about the ecosystem," said Dave Wagner, portfolio manager at Aptus Capital Advisors.

items.title: Tesla expands robotaxi service to Orlando, Tampa ahead of earnings
items.summary: Tesla on Tuesday expanded its robotaxi service to Orlando and Tampa, as ‌the electric-vehicle maker races to prove that ‌it can scale its autonomous ride-hailing business beyond its initial ​launch markets.  The move comes a day before Tesla reports second-quarter earnings, with Wall Street closely watching the progress on robotaxis, which underpin much of the company's ‌valuation as CEO ⁠Elon Musk shifts focus toward artificial intelligence, autonomous driving and humanoid robots.  Tesla launched its ⁠robotaxi service in Austin in June last year and expanded to Dallas and Houston earlier this year ​and ​Miami this month.
